title: IPv4 address spill - leaving NIC unmasqed
body:
### Issue Description

Packets originating from containers leave my hosts network interface that have a source IP set from the containers namespace, in my case 10.89.0.0/24. Those packets arrive at the firewall but when they return to the firewall, they can never be routed back to the container host, since the route does not exist at the firewall.
The result is malfunctioning services.
I just switched to Debian because under Ubuntu 22.04 the problem was really bad. Eversince I switched to Debian the amount of problematic packets shrunk but never went to 0.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I don't know the exact circumstances when this happens but I will try to give as much details about my setup as possible.

# Host
Container Host is virtualized Debian in Proxmox 8, using kvm64 CPU type and 16 GB of RAM with 1 GB of SWAP.

# Software version
- Debian 12.2 6.1.0-13-amd64
- aardvark / netavark 1.4.0-3
- podman 4.3.1
- iptables 1.8.9-2
- nftables / libnftables 1.0.6-2

# Container env
- running around 45 containers
- no firewalld no ufw
- RAM usage about 25%
- Load between 1 and 2#
- 14 cores assigned 
- using netavark / aardvark
- All containers using the podmannet network
```root@cntr-ofden1:~# podman network inspect podmannet
[
     {
          "name": "podmannet",
          "id": "cbea01b7398ff5fdf95faf1a5275d62d0691f0821a7906f4dd36bfe054956b53",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "podman1",
          "created": "2023-11-01T23:35:21.231236772+01:00",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "10.89.0.0/24",
                    "gateway": "10.89.0.1"
               },
               {
                    "subnet": "fda9:92d6:18ac:e10e::/64",
                    "gateway": "fda9:92d6:18ac:e10e::1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": true,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": true,
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          }
     }
]
```

### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Packets with a source IP address from the container network leave the network interface, instead of the masked IP from the host.

comments.body:
I assume only some packages are incorrect an in general the NAT works for most of the traffic?
If this is the case I suggest you update to the latest version as I think https://github.com/containers/netavark/pull/774 would fix it.
Or you just add the rule manually `iptables -A FORWARD -m conntrack --ctstate INVALID -j DROP`

> I assume only some packages are incorrect an in general the NAT works for most of the traffic? If this is the case I suggest you update to the latest version as I think [containers/netavark#774](https://github.com/containers/netavark/pull/774) would fix it. Or you just add the rule manually `iptables -A FORWARD -m conntrack --ctstate INVALID -j DROP`

Yeah only very few packages are incorrect.
I will see what I can do to update. I will report back.
I am confident that this rule will fix the issue so I am going to close this but feel free to comment if it did not help and I will reopen.
